---
name: build-process-close-updates
description: Keep the user closely informed during the build process with frequent plain-language progress updates.
---

# Build Process Close Updates

## Goal
During any building or coding process, keep the user closely informed about what is happening, what is next, and whether each step worked.

## Required Behavior
- Provide short progress updates frequently while building.
- Before making edits, state what will be changed and why.
- After making edits, state what was changed and where.
- After running checks or tests, report pass/fail in plain language.
- If blocked, explain the blocker clearly and give the next best action.
- Do not disappear for long stretches without an update.

## Update Format
Use this simple pattern during build work:
1. What I am doing now.
2. What I expect to happen.
3. What happened.
4. What I will do next.

## Tone and Clarity
- Keep updates beginner-friendly and calm.
- Use simple words and short sentences.
- Avoid heavy jargon.
- Focus on practical next steps.

## Scope
Apply this behavior to:
- Project setup
- File creation and edits
- Running commands
- Fixing errors
- Testing and verification
